top.thesis: PROTHENA CORP PUBLIC LTD CO (PRTA) screens constructively, with 8 independent categories pointing favourably — led by growth, historical analogs, financial health. The weighted composite score is 72/100, and 8 of the scored categories agree on the bullish side versus 2 on the bearish side — conviction here rests on that breadth of agreement, not on any single category. The main offsetting concern is price technicals (score 28/100). This is a research/screening read of independent evidence, not a prediction or investment advice.

top.technical_summary: Category scores — price technicals 28/100 (confidence 100%), momentum 36/100 (confidence 80%). Latest close 8.53 on 2026-07-23; the available 501-bar window ranged 4.32–25.42. The last close sits at the 20% point of that range (0% = window low, 100% = window high).
top.confidence_explanation: Of 11 scored non-risk categories, 8 agree on the bullish side (8 bullish, 2 bearish); signal-agreement is 38% and overall conviction confidence is 39%. Conviction is intentionally driven by how many INDEPENDENT categories agree, not by any single extreme score. Mean data coverage across categories is 83% — the fraction of required inputs that were actually present; missing data lowers, never inflates, the score. Confidence would RISE if: more independent categories moving into agreement. Confidence would FALL if: categories began to disagree, fresh data warnings appeared, or already-strong categories weakened toward neutral.
monitoring_checklist: Next earnings report: confirm revenue and earnings do not deteriorate (vs Q1 2026: revenue $51.1M, net income $32.7M).
Operating-margin trend: watch whether it holds above the latest 50.5%; sustained compression would weaken the quality and value reads.
Insider activity: latest disclosed transaction was a buy by SCULLY WILLIAM P on 2026-06-16; watch for further open-market buys (supportive) or cluster selling (a warning).
Breakout level: a sustained close above 25.42 (the 501-bar window high) would confirm upside technicals; last close was 8.53.
Breakdown level: a sustained close below 4.32 (the 501-bar window low) would invalidate the technical read and warrant reassessment.
Catalyst follow-through: track resolution of 'Catalyst: regulatory approval: 0.23' and whether it plays out as the evidence suggests.

The main offsetting concern is price technicals (score 17/100). This is a research/screening read of independent evidence, not a prediction or investment advice.

top.confidence_explanation: Of 11 scored non-risk categories, 6 agree on the bullish side (6 bullish, 2 bearish); signal-agreement is 41% and overall conviction confidence is 42%. Conviction is intentionally driven by how many INDEPENDENT categories agree, not by any single extreme score. Mean data coverage across categories is 82% — the fraction of required inputs that were actually present; missing data lowers, never inflates, the score. Confidence would RISE if: filling the thin-coverage categories (ownership / insider activity); raising confidence in ownership / insider activity; more independent categories moving into agreement. Confidence would FALL if: categories began to disagree, fresh data warnings appeared, or already-strong categories weakened toward neutral.
monitoring_checklist: Next earnings report: confirm revenue and earnings do not deteriorate (vs FY2025: revenue $163.2M, net income $114.3M).
Operating-margin trend: watch whether it holds above the latest 58.1%; sustained compression would weaken the quality and value reads.
Insider activity: latest disclosed transaction was a other:J by Mognetti Jean-Marie on 2026-03-31; watch for further open-market buys (supportive) or cluster selling (a warning).
Breakout level: a sustained close above 10.43 (the 78-bar window high) would confirm upside technicals; last close was 4.19.
Breakdown level: a sustained close below 3.51 (the 78-bar window low) would invalidate the technical read and warrant reassessment.
Catalyst follow-through: track resolution of 'Catalyst: earnings beat: -0.18' and whether it plays out as the evidence suggests.

top.confidence_explanation: Of 11 scored non-risk categories, 7 agree on the bullish side (7 bullish, 1 bearish); signal-agreement is 48% and overall conviction confidence is 48%. Conviction is intentionally driven by how many INDEPENDENT categories agree, not by any single extreme score. Mean data coverage across categories is 91% — the fraction of required inputs that were actually present; missing data lowers, never inflates, the score. Confidence would RISE if: more independent categories moving into agreement. Confidence would FALL if: categories began to disagree, fresh data warnings appeared, or already-strong categories weakened toward neutral.
monitoring_checklist: Next earnings report: confirm revenue and earnings do not deteriorate (vs Q1 2026: revenue $7.2M, net income $4.0M).
Operating-margin trend: watch whether it holds above the latest 30.2%; sustained compression would weaken the quality and value reads.
Insider activity: latest disclosed transaction was a award by Turgeon Jean Pierre on 2026-07-13; watch for further open-market buys (supportive) or cluster selling (a warning).
Breakout level: a sustained close above 23.27 (the 324-bar window high) would confirm upside technicals; last close was 4.66.
Breakdown level: a sustained close below 1.31 (the 324-bar window low) would invalidate the technical read and warrant reassessment.
Catalyst follow-through: track resolution of 'Catalyst: product launch: 0.2' and whether it plays out as the evidence suggests.

Conviction is intentionally driven by how many INDEPENDENT categories agree, not by any single extreme score. Mean data coverage across categories is 86% — the fraction of required inputs that were actually present; missing data lowers, never inflates, the score. Confidence would RISE if: more independent categories moving into agreement. Confidence would FALL if: categories began to disagree, fresh data warnings appeared, or already-strong categories weakened toward neutral.
